findings.title: Seccomp not enabled on daemon
findings.description: Docker daemon is not configured with seccomp support.
findings.remediation: Set appropriate ulimits: dockerd --default-ulimit nproc=1024:2048 --default-ulimit nofile=100:200

cis_control.title: Ensure the default ulimit is configured appropriately
cis_control.description: Default ulimits control resource usage for containers. Proper limits prevent resource exhaustion attacks.

findings.title: Ensure Userland Proxy is Disabled
findings.description: The userland proxy is slower than hairpin NAT mode and not needed in most environments.
findings.remediation: Disable userland proxy: dockerd --userland-proxy=false or set userland-proxy: false in daemon.json.

cis_control.title: Ensure Userland Proxy is Disabled
cis_control.description: The userland proxy is slower than hairpin NAT mode and not needed in most environments.

findings.title: User namespace remapping not enabled
findings.description: User namespace remapping provides additional isolation by mapping container users to unprivileged host users.
findings.remediation: Configure --userns-remap in daemon.json for additional container isolation.

findings.title: Ensure that a user for the container has been created
findings.description: Running containers as root increases the risk of container escape and host compromise. A dedicated non-root user should be created for the container.
findings.remediation: Add USER instruction to Dockerfile: RUN useradd -r -u 1000 appuser && USER appuser

cis_control.title: Ensure that a user for the container has been created
cis_control.description: Running containers as root increases the risk of container escape and host compromise. A dedicated non-root user should be created for the container.

findings.title: Ensure that HEALTHCHECK instructions have been added to container images
findings.description: Health checks allow Docker to detect when containers become unresponsive and restart them automatically.
findings.remediation: Add HEALTHCHECK instruction to Dockerfile: HEALTHCHECK CMD curl -f http://localhost/ || exit 1

cis_control.title: Ensure that HEALTHCHECK instructions have been added to container images
cis_control.description: Health checks allow Docker to detect when containers become unresponsive and restart them automatically.

findings.title: Image exposes privileged ports: 80
findings.description: Image exposes ports below 1024 which typically require root privileges.
findings.remediation: Consider using non-privileged ports (>1024) and mapping them at runtime if needed.

findings.title: Ensure that a user for the container has been created
findings.description: Running containers as root increases the risk of container escape and host compromise. A dedicated non-root user should be created for the container.
findings.remediation: Add USER instruction to Dockerfile: RUN useradd -r -u 1000 appuser && USER appuser

cis_control.title: Ensure that a user for the container has been created
cis_control.description: Running containers as root increases the risk of container escape and host compromise. A dedicated non-root user should be created for the container.
